label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 9

extraction.one_line_summary: KÄMNA Lennard attacked with 4.8km remaining on the final climb, soloing to victory after Jumbo-Visma accidentally infiltrated early breakaway attempts.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma accidentally positioned six riders in early break, forcing peloton reorganization—reveals protective depth backfired into tactical confusion.
Organizers declared GC finish 2km early due to rain/mud, eliminating bonus seconds incentive and neutering breakaway chase motivation.
EVENEPOEL Remco tested ROGLIČ on final climb despite neutralized GC finish, creating seconds gap—probing weakness ahead of time trial.
VLASOV Aleksandr joined late GC attacks targeting top-10 tightness, suggesting borderline GC riders prioritize small gaps pre-rest day.

rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: Attacked solo 4.8km out on 20-23% gradients, completing Grand Tour stage win set after Giro and Tour victories.
rider_signals.HAMILTON Chris: neutral: First to respond to KÄMNA's attack for DSM but lacked reserves to sustain pursuit on steep final ramps.
rider_signals.SOBRERO Matteo: positive: Finished second from breakaway, showing climbing capacity beyond typical time trial specialist profile.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Attacked ROGLIČ despite neutralized GC finish, gaining couple seconds—testing form aggressively before time trial.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: Initiated bonus second attack before GC finish line declaration, demonstrating awareness of tight top-10 gaps.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Responded to late attacks targeting top-10 positioning, recognizing compressed GC standings require opportunistic seconds.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: negative: Caught out in crosswind splits along with group Arkéa, showing vulnerability when peloton fragments in wind.
